runtimeBoundaries.name: legacy_narrow_worker
runtimeBoundaries.scope: Legacy narrow session shell kept as compatibility scaffolding. It remains isolated from DataFusion and is not the Daxis default read-compute worker SKU.

runtimeBoundaries.name: daxis_datafusion_default
runtimeBoundaries.scope: Daxis-facing browser DataFusion worker for descriptor-backed Delta/Parquet reads. It must not route through the legacy narrow session.

removalPlan.requirement: Keep new Daxis-facing capability work on the axon-web-wasm browser DataFusion path while preserving the narrow worker only as an isolated compatibility scaffold.
removalPlan.legacyNarrowRuntimeAction: Do not add Daxis descriptor, read-access-plan, object-grant, or query-corpus capability to the legacy narrow worker path.

removalPlan.requirement: Promote Daxis rollout only after the DataFusion worker artifact report, Daxis query corpus, budget gates, and browser matrix coverage cover Daxis-critical open/query/cancel flows.
removalPlan.legacyNarrowRuntimeAction: Remove the narrow worker from Daxis routing decisions or leave it behind an explicit non-Daxis compatibility switch.

removalPlan.requirement: Require repeatable release evidence, production telemetry, fallback dashboards, oncall runbooks, and tenant or workspace rollout controls before Daxis treats Axon as stable default read compute.
removalPlan.legacyNarrowRuntimeAction: Delete the legacy narrow runtime or keep it only as a documented fixture/compatibility target with no production Daxis routing.

promotionRequirements: Daxis query corpus passes through browser DataFusion in-memory and descriptor-backed paths.
Daxis budget profile stays inside the documented scan, output, row, artifact, startup, and memory envelopes.
The Axon worker artifact report names browser DataFusion as the Daxis default runtime SKU before Daxis default routing changes.
Browser matrix coverage includes Daxis-critical descriptor open, query, cancel, unsupported-feature, and worker-terminal-error flows.
Daxis production dashboards and oncall runbooks exist before stable default routing.
notes: This plan closes the M3 removal-or-isolation planning gap for the Axon repo by making axon-web-wasm the Daxis-facing DataFusion worker SKU.
The narrow worker remains a compatibility scaffold with no Daxis routing claim.
M4 production dashboards, oncall runbooks, tenant rollout controls, and compatibility dashboards remain outside this repo-owned isolation plan.
